what_it_does: Acceptance test suite for the inward-facing company noticeboard (cc_board): it files typed items, lists and retrieves them, transitions them along per-type registry lifecycles, validates every type/state/source/edge reference against registries, and proves a new edge-kind can be added as a registry row without code changes.
observations: Self-contained plain-assert harness with PASS/FAIL accumulators and a simple check() printer
Imports runtime.cc_board as cb via sys.path manipulation
Creates a temporary board_dir and tests id-keyed flat storage (<id>.md) with type in frontmatter
Validates registry-backed item types, source types, and edge kinds, and rejects unregistered values fail-loud
Tests canonical address board://<id> and the rule that identity contains nothing mutable
Tests per-type lifecycle via cb.legal_transitions and cb.initial_state
Records and checks transition history including from/to/by/note
Tests typed edges as [{kind,target}] validated against the edge-kind registry
Writes a temporary blocks.py edge-kind file into cb.BOARD_EDGES_DIR, calls cb.reset_registries(), and verifies the new kind is usable
Cleans up the temporary edge-kind file in a finally block
standouts: Fail-loud validation of every registry reference is treated as a first-class requirement
Extensibility is proven by adding a new edge-kind file at runtime and rediscovering it
Flat identity design (board://<id> and <id>.md) is explicitly tested against per-type subdirectories
conventions: Docstring doubles as design rationale and run instructions
Uses module-level mutable PASS/FAIL lists and a tmp directory variable
raises() helper only catches cb.BoardError, treating other exceptions as False
concerns: The test writes into a source-managed registry directory (cb.BOARD_EDGES_DIR) at runtime; if the finally cleanup fails, repository state is altered
Global PASS/FAIL lists and tmp are module-level mutable state
raises() swallows non-BoardError exceptions without diagnostics
Test 25 depends on BOARD_EDGES_DIR being writable and isolated, which is not guaranteed by the file alone
notes: A falsify-first acceptance floor for the company noticeboard that treats registry rows as the single source of truth for types, states, sources, and edge kinds.
questions: Where are the registry files physically located and what schema do they follow?
Does cb.reset_registries() safely handle concurrent access or partially written files?
What is the exact frontmatter format stored in each <id>.md file?
How does the test environment ensure BOARD_EDGES_DIR is writable and isolated?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose and actual content align: it is an acceptance suite that exercises registry-driven filing, listing, transition, validation, and row-add extensibility for the noticeboard.
apparent_themes: Flat, immutable identity for mutable-typed records
intention_signals: The file is trying to lock in architectural decisions for the noticeboard: every categorical value is a registry row, identity is flat and type-agnostic, lifecycles are per-type, and the edge vocabulary can grow by adding data files rather than editing code.
novelty: Explicitly tests that a new edge-kind can be introduced by adding a registry row at runtime and immediately used, making the registry mechanism itself part of the acceptance surface.
contribution: A runnable acceptance contract that documents and guards the noticeboard's data model: flat board://<id> identity, per-type lifecycles, registry-validated references, typed edges, and row-add extensibility.
summary_for_embedding: Plain-assert acceptance test for the company noticeboard that exercises filing, listing, transitioning, and validating registry-backed items with typed edges and per-type lifecycles.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a falsify-first acceptance floor proving the noticeboard's registry-driven design and runtime edge-kind extensibility.

symbols.does: Records a named assertion result in PASS or FAIL and prints a PASS/FAIL line.
symbols.description: The check helper is the test harness's assertion primitive: it takes a name, a boolean condition, and an optional diagnostic string, appends the name to PASS or FAIL, and prints a formatted result line used by the plain-assert runner.

symbols.does: Returns True if a callable raises cb.BoardError (optionally containing a substring), otherwise False.
symbols.description: The raises helper supports the fail-loud validation checks by attempting a callable and checking whether it raises a BoardError with an expected message fragment; it returns False for non-raising calls or non-BoardError exceptions.
